settings.text:
At Squawk Shop, we do not leave you guessing what your Java Tree will look like. We have heard way too many horror stories of people spending a fortune and ending up with a tree they are not happy with.

So we took the guesswork out of it.

Every Java Tree Playstand is a true one of one. The photos you see are of the real tree you will receive, and the Tree ID you choose is the exact stand that ships to your door. Pick the shape, branch layout, and overall vibe that fits your home and your flock, then select your Tree ID on the product page to lock it in.

Made from real Java or coffee wood, these stands are built for daily parrot life: perching, climbing, stretching, and hanging out like they own the place.

settings.heading: How to choose the right Java Tree?

settings.text:
Every Java Wood tree is a masterpiece of nature—no two are identical. To find the perfect match for your parrot's personality and your home, follow this 5-step curator’s guide:

1. Species-Specific Scaling

  • Small & Medium Birds: Look for "ladder-style" branch configurations with tighter spacing for easy hopping.
  • Large Parrots (Macaws/Cockatoos): Prioritize heavy-duty, thicker diameters and wider spacing to accommodate their larger wingspans and climbing needs.
  • Confidence Factor: For timid birds, choose a tree with dense branch clusters to help them feel secure while navigating.

2. Match the "Branch Energy" to Their Personality

  • The Explorer: Look for "High Chaos" trees with intersecting branches and multiple routes. Perfect for high-energy birds that love to climb and forage.
  • The Lounger: Select a tree with a prominent, thick main perch and open access. Best for birds that prefer to survey their kingdom from one comfortable spot.
  • The High-Flyer: Choose a taller profile with a clear "King of the Hill" top perch for birds that insist on being the highest point in the room.

3. Spatial Design & Footprint

Measure your space before you buy! If the tree will live against a wall, look for an asymmetrical growth pattern where branches lean toward one side. For center-room "islands," a balanced spread allows for 360° access.

4. Consider the "Drop Zone"

While our bases provide excellent coverage, remember:

  • Wide Spread: Offers more enrichment area but requires a larger "scatter zone" for debris.
  • Compact Spread: Keeps mess more contained—ideal for smaller rooms or apartments.

5. Claim Your Unique Tree ID

Once you find "the one," select the specific Tree ID on the product page. That ID is linked to the exact, unique tree pictured—what you see is exactly what will arrive at your door.

Don’t see the perfect branch for your flock?

Our online gallery is just the beginning. Because we work directly with A&E Cage Co., we have access to a massive "unlisted" inventory that changes daily.

If you’re looking for a specific height, a particular branch density, or a "statement piece" for a large macaw, we can find it for you.

Live Chat: Hit the chat icon to speak with a a member of our team right now.

Email Us: Reach out to contact@squawkshop.com to request our Full Current Catalog with photos of our latest unlisted arrivals.
